content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE

CITY ATTORNEY

November 17, 1992


Louisa Menchaka
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Re: City of South San Francisco El Camino Corridor Redevelopment
     Project

Dear Ms. Menchaka:

     This letter is a request for advice concerning several potential
conflicts of interest involving South San Francisco's El Camino
Corridor Redevelopment Project.

                              BACKGROUND

     The City of South San Francisco is currently in the process of
creating a redevelopment area and project along the El Camino
Corridor in the City.  The City has a five member Council which
also serves on the Redevelopment Agency.  In previous discussions
with your office, it was determined that three Councilmembers have
potential conflicts of interest.

     The potential conflicts of interest are as follows:

     1.   Mayor Penna has an active real estate license in the City
          of South San Francisco and routinely receives income from
          the sale of property in the City's limits.  Reference
          Opinion letters A-90-499b and A-90-499.

          Also, Mayor Penna's residence is located within 2500 feet
          of the proposed project area.

     2.   Councilman Drago possesses a real estate license and
          received a fee for utilizing it on one occasion.  Reference
          Advice Letter I-91-516.

          Councilman Drago's residence is within 2500 feet of the
          proposed redevelopment area.




                         315 Maple Avenue 94080
     City Hall Annex      P.O. Box 711 94083        (415) 877-8515

Louisa Menchaka
Fair Political Practice Commission
November 17, 1992
Page 2

     3.     Councilwoman Teglia's residence is located within 300
            feet of the proposed redevelopment area.

The El Camino Redevelopment Area runs along El Camino Real in the
City.  There are approximately 1140 residences within 300 feet of
the area and approximately 7300 residences within 2500 feet of the
area.  According to the most recent census date, South San
Francisco has approximately 54,312 residents with a population per
dwelling ratio of 2.8 persons per one unit.  The approximate total
number of dwelling units in the City is 19,081.  Based upon these
facts the following estimates have been drawn.

     o    5.9% of the City's dwelling units are within 300 feet of
          the El Camino Project Area.

     o    5.9% of the City's population resides within 300 feet of
          the Project Area.

     o    38% of the City's dwelling units are within 2500 feet of
          the Project Area.

     o    37.9% of the City's population resides within 2500 feet
          of the Project Area.

Since this matter is in the early stages there are numerous
upcoming discussions for both the City Council and Redevelopment
Agency to act upon.  Considering that three council members were
advised to abstain on the adoption of a survey area, it was
necessary to conduct a drawing of lots to determine which of the
abstaining members would participate to ensure a quorum.
Councilwoman Teglia was chosen as the participating member.

                            QUESTIONS

1.   Councilwoman Teglia.

     a.   Since approximately 6% of the City's residences and
          population reside 300 feet of the proposed project area
          and since Councilwoman Teglia's house will not be
          affected any differently from the residences of 6% of the
          population, does she qualify for the "public generally"
          exception with respect to the potential conflict
          concerning the location of her residence?

2.   Use of the Lottery System to choose a participating abstainer.

Louisa Menchaka
Fair Political Practice Commission
November 17, 1992
Page 3


a)  Since Councilwoman Teglia has drawn the lot and
participated as a Councilwoman in approving the survey
area is it necessary to hold a new lottery when the
Redevelopment Agency acts upon the project area or does
the drawing of lots for Councilmembers carry over to
their Agency actions? If it is not necessary to draw new
lots, is it permissible?

b)  If all of the conflicts remain the same, which of the
following Council/Agency actions would require or allow
a new lottery and which would require the continued
participation of Councilwoman Teglia who was selected to
vote on the survey area at the Council level?

(1)  Agency Actions.

a.  Ratification of Preliminary Plan.

b.  Authorization To Proceed on Plan, prepare
environmental documents and Preliminary
Report.

c.  Agency designation of Lead and Responsible
Agency for environmental documents.

d.  Agency determination that a statutory Project
Area Committee is not required and calling for
community consultations.

e.  Agency authorizing transmittal of Preliminary
Report to taxing agencies.

f.  Agency decisions on E.I.R.

g.  Agency transmittal of Final Plan, E.I.R. and
Report to Council.

h.  Agency consent to holding a joint public
hearing with the City Council on proposed
Redevelopment Plan.

i.  Agency adoption of Rules for Owner
Participation and Tenant Preference.

j.  Agency adoption of State Relocation
Guidelines.

Louisa Menchaka
Fair Political Practice Commission
November 17, 1992
Page 4

(2) Council Actions - I have deleted numerous City
Council actions since they are identical to the
Redevelopment Agency actions. Thus, once the
Agency/Council issue on dual lotteries is answered,
I will apply your guidance on Agency issues to the
corresponding Council issues.

a. City call for fiscal review committee.

b. Adoption of resolution electing to receive
portion of tax increment.

c. Adoption of General Plan Amendment.

d. Adopt Zoning Amendments.

e. Adoption of Final Redevelopment Plan.

If you need additional information feel free to contact me at (415)
877-8515. I appreciate your timely response to this matter as the
above-discussed actions will begin towards the end of November.

Yours very truly,

Michael J. O'Toole
City Attorney

MJO/mm

sections.question:
1. Councilwoman Teglia.

a. Since approximately 6% of the City's residences and population reside 300 feet of the proposed project area and since Councilwoman Teglia's house will not be affected any differently from the residences of 6% of the population, does she qualify for the "public generally" exception with respect to the potential conflict concerning the location of her residence?

2. Use of the Lottery System to choose a participating abstainer.

a) Since Councilwoman Teglia has drawn the lot and participated as a Councilwoman in approving the survey area is it necessary to hold a new lottery when the Redevelopment Agency acts upon the project area or does the drawing of lots for Councilmembers carry over to their Agency actions? If it is not necessary to draw new lots, is it permissible?

b) If all of the conflicts remain the same, which of the following Council/Agency actions would require or allow a new lottery and which would require the continued participation of Councilwoman Teglia who was selected to vote on the survey area at the Council level?

(1) Agency Actions.

    a. Ratification of Preliminary Plan.

    b. Authorization To Proceed on Plan, prepare environmental documents and Preliminary Report.

    c. Agency designation of Lead and Responsible Agency for environmental documents.

    d. Agency determination that a statutory Project Area Committee is not required and calling for community consultations.

    e. Agency authorizing transmittal of Preliminary Report to taxing agencies.

    f. Agency decisions on E.I.R.

    g. Agency transmittal of Final Plan, E.I.R. and Report to Council.

    h. Agency consent to holding a joint public hearing with the City Council on proposed Redevelopment Plan.

    i. Agency adoption of Rules for Owner Participation and Tenant Preference.

    j. Agency adoption of State Relocation Guidelines.

Louisa Menchaka  
Fair Political Practice Commission  
November 17, 1992  

(2) Council Actions - I have deleted numerous City Council actions since they are identical to the Redevelopment Agency actions. Thus, once the Agency/Council issue on dual lotteries is answered, I will apply your guidance on Agency issues to the corresponding Council issues.

a. City call for fiscal review committee.

b. Adoption of resolution electing to receive portion of tax increment.

c. Adoption of General Plan Amendment.

d. Adopt Zoning Amendments.

e. Adoption of Final Redevelopment Plan.

If you need additional information feel free to contact me at (415) 877-8515. I appreciate your timely response to this matter as the above-discussed actions will begin towards the end of November.

Yours very truly,

[Signature]
Michael J. O'Toole  
City Attorney

MJO/mm

The City of South San Francisco is currently in the process of creating a redevelopment area and project along the El Camino Corridor in the City. The City has a five member Council which also serves on the Redevelopment Agency. In previous discussions with your office, it was determined that three Councilmembers have potential conflicts of interest.

The potential conflicts of interest are as follows:

1. Mayor Penna has an active real estate license in the City of South San Francisco and routinely receives income from the sale of property in the City’s limits. Reference Opinion letters A-90-499b and A-90-499.

Also, Mayor Penna’s residence is located within 2500 feet of the proposed project area.

2. Councilman Drago possesses a real estate license and received a fee for utilizing it on one occasion. Reference Advice Letter I-91-516.

Councilman Drago’s residence is within 2500 feet of the proposed redevelopment area.

Louisa Menchaka
Fair Political Practice Commission
November 17, 1992

3. Councilwoman Teglia's residence is located within 300 feet of the proposed redevelopment area.

The El Camino Redevelopment Area runs along El Camino Real in the City. There are approximately 1140 residences within 300 feet of the area and approximately 7300 residences within 2500 feet of the area. According to the most recent census date, South San Francisco has approximately 54,312 residents with a population per dwelling ratio of 2.8 persons per one unit. The approximate total number of dwelling units in the City is 19,081. Based upon these facts the following estimates have been drawn.

° 5.9% of the City's dwelling units are within 300 feet of the El Camino Project Area.

° 5.9% of the City's population resides within 300 feet of the Project Area.

° 38% of the City's dwelling units are within 2500 feet of the Project Area.

° 37.9% of the City's population resides within 2500 feet of the Project Area.

Since this matter is in the early stages there are numerous upcoming discussions for both the City Council and Redevelopment Agency to act upon. Considering that three Councilmembers were advised to abstain on the adoption of a survey area, it was necessary to conduct a drawing of lots to determine which of the abstaining members would participate to assure a quorum. Councilwoman Teglia was chosen as the participating member.
